---
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

Please find the full CV [here](./cv_eyayaw_beze.pdf).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## About

<a href="https://www.github.com/eyayaw/"> <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/eyayaw-blue?style=flat&amp;logo=github" alt="Static Badge"/> </a> <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/eyayaw/"> <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/eyayaw-blue?style=flat&amp;logo=linkedin" alt="Static Badge"/> </a> <a href="mailto:eyayaw.beze@gmail.com"> <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/eyayaw.beze-blue?style=flat&amp;logo=Gmail" alt="Static Badge"/> </a>

**Ph.D. candidate in Economics**\
**R and Python enthusiast \| Urban Economics \| Housing Markets \| Data Science**

::: callout
A strong background in econometrics, statistics, and data science. I am interested in the application of data science and econometrics in the field of urban economics and housing markets. I have experience in working with large datasets, data cleaning, data visualization, and data analysis. I am proficient in R, Python, and SQL.
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Work Experience

-   Scientific researcher at the Chair of Economics, University of Duisburg-Essen
-   Intern and RA at IOS Regensburg
-   Lecturer and ICT coordinator at Jimma University

## Education

-   Ph.D. candidate in Economics, Ruhr Graduate School in Economics
-   MA in Comparative Local Development, University of {Corvinus, Regensburg, Trento, Lijubljana}
-   MA in Development Policy, KDI School of Public Policy and Management
-   BA in Economics, Bahir Dar University

## Dissertation

Title: **Essays on Housing Supply and the Monocentric City Model**\

1.  [**Testing the Gradient Predictions of the Monocentric City Model in Addis Ababa**](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4803607) \</[code](https://github.com/eyayaw/the-monocentric-city-gradients-addis-ababa)\>

2.  [**The impact of COVID-19 on real estate markets in Germany.**](https://ssrn.com/abstract=4700238) \</[code](https://github.com/eyayaw/de-donut-effect)\>

3.  [**Geographic Constraints and the Housing Supply Elasticity in Germany**](https://uni-duisburg-essen.sciebo.de/s/km7tQzcygRQjwp6). \</[code](https://github.com/eyayaw/housing-supply-elasticity-in-germany)\>

## Skills

-   R, Python, Stata, Zsh, Git, MySQL, GIS, LaTeX/Quarto/Markdown, Shiny

## Interests

-   Data, web scraping, football
